prompt: Use miniprogram-design-qa to validate a WeChat mini-program support page against a local baseline screenshot.
why: Native mini-program acceptance with local design baseline.

prompt: Compare a WeChat mini-program detail page against a design screenshot and produce a QA report.
Run runtime-only acceptance for a WeChat mini-program page with no design reference.
why: Runtime-only mini-program QA is in scope.

prompt: Validate a Taro weapp page in WeChat DevTools and classify visual findings.
why: Taro weapp is a supported first-class target.

prompt: The scenario contains figmaFileKey and figmaNodeId, but no local design image. Use the metadata and still run runtime acceptance.
why: Figma metadata may exist in scenarios even though built-in compare still needs a local image.

prompt: Check a browser-only React admin page with Playwright screenshots.
why: Browser-only page should use a browser-focused QA workflow instead.

prompt: Review a marketing landing page in Chrome and compare against Figma.
why: This repo is not the primary skill for browser-only surfaces.

prompt: Validate an iOS SwiftUI settings screen from simulator screenshots.
why: Native mobile is out of the first-class support target for this repo.

prompt: Run WeChat mini-program QA on a tabbed support review page using query.tab=manual for state validation.
why: Route/query-driven tab state validation is a supported mini-program QA pattern.

prompt: Run compare on local screenshots only, no Figma, no runtime capture.
why: The repo centers on runtime evidence; compare-only image utilities may be used directly, but the full skill should not be the default trigger.
